teachingPoint: JSON Schema validation must occur BEFORE instantiation to prevent malformed template inheritance
positiveExample: draft → validate → lock → publish ensures schema immutability on all clones
negativeExample: instantiate → validate → publish allows invalid schema to propagate to multiple forms
discriminatingConstraint: Validation gate is atomic; invalid schemas fail the entire batch
keywords: JSON schema validation, immutable templates, batch failure atomicity

teachingPoint: OCC (Optimistic Concurrency Control) DRAFT→PUBLISHED state machine prevents publish race conditions
positiveExample: versionNumber pinned at DRAFT; publish increments and locks; clones reference published version only
negativeExample: updating published template without version check allows stale clones to inherit broken schema
discriminatingConstraint: WHERE versionNumber=old on publish; conflict returns CONFLICT, never FAILURE
keywords: OCC versionNumber, DRAFT state, PUBLISHED lock, conflict handling

teachingPoint: SETNX instantiation lock prevents simultaneous form creation from same template
positiveExample: SETNX lock__{templateId}__{userId} → create form → DEL lock guarantees one active instantiation per user per template
negativeExample: concurrent instantiation without SETNX causes duplicate form records + inventory mismatch
discriminatingConstraint: Lock must be set BEFORE storeDocument; lock release AFTER enqueue completes
keywords: SETNX lock, form instantiation, user lock-scoped, DEL cleanup

teachingPoint: Append-only analytics logs must never store PII or user-identifying schema field mappings
positiveExample: Record: {instanceId, templateId, createdAt, fieldCount, completionSeconds} — no field names, no values
negativeExample: Recording {fieldName: 'social_security', submittedValue: sanitized_value} leaks schema intent to analytics
discriminatingConstraint: Analytics records use hash(field_position) only; content hashing forbidden for compliance
keywords: append-only logs, PII-free analytics, field position hashing, compliance boundary

teachingPoint: Pure computation (JSON validation, schema rendering) MUST NOT call external services or async fabric methods
positiveExample: validateSchema(template: Record<string, unknown>): boolean — pure function, zero dependencies, cacheable
negativeExample: validateSchema async calling RAG or AI fabric breaks composition and causes timeouts
discriminatingConstraint: Service-layer async validation; pure validators are stateless, side-effect-free, parallelizable
keywords: pure validators, zero dependencies, cacheability, parallelization

teachingPoint: READ-ONLY context rendering prevents modification of template structure during form preview/generation
positiveExample: renderTemplate(template: Readonly<Record<string, unknown>>, context: Readonly<...>) — TypeScript Readonly ensures compile-time immutability
negativeExample: renderTemplate(template) { template.fields.push(...) } allows unintended schema mutation
discriminatingConstraint: All rendering methods accept Readonly<T>; return new object; never mutate input
keywords: Readonly<T>, template immutability, new object returns, mutation prevention

description: Validates template JSON schema before any state transition; errors fail atomic
observedIn: T632 validateTemplateSchema receives template, returns validation result or INVALID_SCHEMA error
ironRules: All fields required in schema properties
codeSnippet: const schema = template.schema; const valid = ajv.validate(schema, {}); if(!valid) return failure('INVALID_SCHEMA');
keywords: JSON schema, ajv validation, atomic gate, error accumulation

description: OCC for template lifecycle: DRAFT (editable) → PUBLISHED (immutable, cloneable)
observedIn: T632 uses versionNumber; T633 publishes with WHERE clause; T634 clones reference publishedVersionId
ironRules: DRAFT templates have versionNumber; publish increments it
codeSnippet: await db.updateDocument({where: {templateId, versionNumber: old}}, {status: 'PUBLISHED', versionNumber: new});
keywords: versionNumber OCC, DRAFT editable, PUBLISHED immutable, clone references

description: Redis SETNX prevents simultaneous form instantiation from same template by same user
observedIn: T634 acquires SETNX lock__{{templateId}}__{{userId}} before instantiation; DEL on completion
ironRules: Lock key includes both templateId and userId
codeSnippet: const locked = await redis.setNX(`lock_${templateId}_${userId}`, '1', 'EX', 30); if(!locked) return failure('INSTANTIATION_IN_PROGRESS');
keywords: Redis SETNX, instantiation lock, user-scoped, EX 30 seconds

description: Analytics logs append form usage metrics without storing field names, values, or identifying schema details
observedIn: T635 records {instanceId, templateId, fieldCount, completionSeconds, submittedAt} — never field content
ironRules: Only aggregate metrics allowed: counts, durations, timestamps
Content hashing for compliance audits only
codeSnippet: await analytics.append({instanceId, templateId, fieldCount: fields.length, submittedAt: now});
keywords: append-only logs, aggregate metrics, PII-free, compliance-safe
